basicAuthUser.description: The username of the data source account

keepCookies.description: Grafana proxy deletes forwarded cookies by default. Specify cookies by name that should be forwarded to the data source.

nodeGraph.description: Show or hide the node graph visualization.

serverName.description: A Servername is used to verify the hostname on the returned certificate

spanBar.description: Add additional info next to the service and operation on a span bar row in the trace view.

tlsAuth.description: Validate using TLS client authentication, in which the server authenticates the client

tlsAuthWithCACert.description: Add your own Certificate Authority (CA) certificate on top of one generated by the certificate authorities for additional security measures

tlsSkipVerify.description: Skipping TLS certificate validation is not recommended unless absolutely necessary or for testing

tracesToLogs.description: Legacy v1 shape for Trace to logs. `getTraceToLogsOptions` (packages/grafana-o11y-ds-frontend/src/TraceToLogs/TraceToLogsSettings.tsx) migrates this to `tracesToLogsV2` on read; the editor clears this key whenever `tracesToLogsV2` is written. Retained here for round-trip compatibility of legacy datasources.

tracesToLogsV2.description: Navigate from a trace span to the selected data source's logs.

tracesToMetrics.description: Navigate from a trace span to the selected data source's metrics.

secureValues.description: The password of the data source account
The client certificate can be generated from a Certificate Authority or be self-signed
The client key can be generated from a Certificate Authority or be self-signed

examples.description: The defaults a new datasource starts with: no authentication, no TLS overrides, no trace-to-X mappings, node graph disabled. Only root.url needs to be filled in to get a working datasource pointed at a Zipkin v2 API on localhost:9411.

basicAuth.description: Authenticate with a Zipkin that requires HTTP Basic. Both basicAuth and basicAuthUser live at the datasource root; only basicAuthPassword is secret. The SDK's HTTPClientOptions wires the credentials automatically (pkg/zipkin/zipkin.go:23).

fullObservability.description: Zipkin wired to Loki (logs) and Prometheus (metrics). Uses the v2 tracesToLogs shape; nodeGraph and spanBar='Duration' are enabled. Note that Zipkin, unlike Tempo, has no trace-to-profiles or service-graph section in the editor, and unlike Jaeger has no Query Trace by ID Time Params toggle.

legacyTracesToLogs.description: A datasource stored before the v2 shape landed. `getTraceToLogsOptions` transforms tracesToLogs → tracesToLogsV2 at editor load; the next save wipes tracesToLogs entirely. Kept as an example so provisioning tooling knows the shape is still round-trippable.

noAuth.description: A public or network-isolated Zipkin with no HTTP-level auth. Node graph is enabled for a richer trace view.

oauthForward.description: Forward the signed-in user's upstream OAuth identity to Zipkin. The editor writes only jsonData.oauthPassThru; there is no accompanying secret.

tlsMutualAuth.description: Zipkin requiring mTLS. jsonData.tlsAuth=true triggers the SDK to build a client-authenticated transport using serverName plus the PEM-encoded client cert and key in secureJsonData.

secureJsonData.tlsClientKey:
-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
...
-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----

tlsSelfSignedCA.description: Zipkin behind a private CA. jsonData.tlsAuthWithCACert=true tells the SDK to verify the server certificate against the PEM-encoded CA in secureJsonData.tlsCACert.
